title: Designing for constraint: TFLite Micro on a $4 board
preview: What happens when you have 400 KB of RAM, a model that needs 60 KB of tensor arena, and I2S DMA running simultaneously...

detail.body:
The ESP32-C3 Mini has 400 KB of SRAM total. By the time you account for the Arduino stack (~8 KB), I2S DMA buffers (~8 KB), and audio ring buffers (~4 KB), you have about 380 KB left. Our TFLite Micro model needs 50 KB of tensor arena minimum. That sounds comfortable until you realize the firmware code itself also lives in SRAM during execution.

The key lesson: always profile memory before writing a single line of ML inference code. On microcontrollers, the model is the last thing you fit in — everything else gets allocated first.

We ended up reducing the tensor arena from 60 KB (S3 target) to 50 KB and trimming the model's conv layer filters from 32 to 24, losing about 2% test accuracy but gaining stable execution with zero OOM crashes.
